_comment: Illustrative: how the Enclave PEP wires into a headless Claude Code session. Exact field names track the current Claude Code docs; the mechanism (deny-by-default + a PreToolUse hook that gates every call) is the stable part.

permissions._note: In production these live in *managed* settings so neither the user nor the model can widen them.

_run: Set ENCLAVE_SESSION to the signed session file before launching, e.g.:  ENCLAVE_SESSION=./session/dana.json claude -p "..."  — the hook fires before every tool call and returns allow/deny.
_production: In production the hook does not evaluate Cedar in-process; it forwards (bound-identity, action, resource, context) to the PDP service over mTLS and enforces the verdict. See ../../docs/security-architecture.md §5 and §10.
